calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-30-2018 Off Calendar

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-12-2018 Continued To Sep-11-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
MINI MINUTES:
David Serrano, Esq. appearing for the defendant and specially appearing for the plaintiff. The Order To Show Cause is re-set to SEP-11-2018 at 10:30am in Department 610. Judge: Teri L. Jackson; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Proceedings not reported.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-09-2018 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jul-10-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order
Off Calendar Dec-12-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Carey Wood of the law offices of Strategic Legal Practices (310-929-4900) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, David A. Serrano of the law offices of Gordon & Rees LLP (415-986-5900) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling with modification.

DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY's Motion For Protective Order is granted in part. A protective order that has the same terms and conditions as the confidentiality protective order entered in the Southern California JCCP cases will be entered in this and all other Ford DPS6 cases now pending or filed in the future in San Francisco Superior Court. There does not appear to be any persuasive reason why cases filed in San Francisco should receive different confidentiality information treatment than cases that are filed in Southern California. From now on, counsel in Ford DPS6 cases are expected to submit stipulated protective orders including the same terms and conditions as the confidentiality protective order entered in the Southern California JCCP cases as soon as possible for pending cases and soon after all defendants have appeared in newly filed cases. For those cases that a different protective order has previously been entered, counsel are expected to submit stipulated amended protective orders which expressly state that they supersede the prior orders. Counsel in all Ford DPS6 cases are required to make every reasonable effort to avoid filing motions of any kind raising issues that have already been adjudicated in this court in another Ford DPS6 case. Counsel for the parties to this case are required to notify counsel in all other Ford DPS6 cases now pending or filed in the future in San Francisco Superior Court of this ruling.
A protective order is signed and filed in open court. Document responses are due by January 22, 2018. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling on the motion, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR No. 11185, e-mail: adelgado@sftc.org (L&M302)

Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents Set One
Off Calendar Dec-12-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Carey Wood of the law offices of Strategic Legal Practices (310-929-4900) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, David A. Serrano of the law offices of Gordon & Rees LLP (415-986-5900) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling with modification.

PLAINTIFF ANGELICA JIMENEZ's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents Set One is granted. Contingent on the court entering a confidentiality protective order in this case by December 15, 2017, no later than January 29, 2018 Ford is required to provide further responses to Ms. Jimenez' set one requests. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR No. 11185, e-mail: adelgado@sftc.org (L&M302)
